metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Other
Competitive Agreeableness (31st pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Highly conscientious Conscientiousness (90th pct) — Disciplined, organised, dependable, goal-directed
Extraverted Extraversion (70th pct) — Extraverted, outgoing, energetic
Highly neurotic Neuroticism (87th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Highly open Openness to Experience (81st pct) — Open, curious, imaginative, creative
  Low Tender-Mindedness (32nd pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Very High Achievement Striving (89th pct) — Ambitious, driven, works hard toward goals
  Very High Self-Discipline (94th pct) — Self-disciplined, persistent, finishes tasks
  High Activity Level (61st pct) — Fast-paced, energetic, busy
  High Excitement Seeking (72nd pct) — Thrill-seeking, excitement-hungry
  High Positive Emotions (74th pct) — Joyful, enthusiastic, optimistic
  Very High Anger / Hostility (80th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  Very High Self-Consciousness (84th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Highly neurotic Neuroticism (81st pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Low Chronological Age (36th pct)

message.reasoning:
We are simulating a baseline opinion for a policy item: "Trust In Mainstream Journalism". The profile shows high neuroticism (especially anxiety, anger/hostility), low agreeableness (especially trust at 26th percentile, straightforwardness low, compliance low, modesty low), high conscientiousness, high openness to experience. The person is Other sex, 36 years old.

Key traits: low trust (Agreeableness trust 26%), high neuroticism (anxiety 93%, anger 80%), high openness (ideas 95%, values 85%), high conscientiousness (deliberation 98%, dutifulness 92%). They are skeptical, critical, and likely have low trust in institutions. They are high in openness, so they may be open to alternative sources, but also high in neuroticism may make them anxious about information integrity. Their low trust and high anger/hostility suggest they are likely distrustful of mainstream journalism. They are also highly conscientious and deliberative, so they might base their opinion on careful reasoning, but given low trust, they probably lean toward distrust. However, they are not extreme in all dimensions; they have moderate extraversion and positive emotions. But overall, the profile suggests a fairly strong distrust of mainstream journalism. The anchor descriptions: -500 "Clear opposition: disagrees on balance, with some acknowledged trade-offs" might fit. But could be stronger? Let's consider distribution guidelines: ~20% strongly leaning (600-850). I think this profile could be in that range. But careful: they are open to ideas and values, so they might be skeptical but not completely closed off. However, low trust specifically points to distrust. I'll go with -670 to indicate strong opposition but not maximal. Also, they have high anger/hostility which might amplify. Use a non-round number: -673. Confidence: 0.78. Reasoning: Low Agreeableness (trust 26th pct) and high Neuroticism (anxiety, anger) contribute to fundamental skepticism toward institutional information sources; high Openness suggests openness to alternative narratives, reinforcing distrust of mainstream.
